overview: Adds or removes the Unicode Byte-Order-Mark (BOM) to or from UTF-8 and UTF-16 input files. The BOM is a special Unicode mark indicating byte order: little-endian (U+FFFE) or big-endian (U+FEFF). This step operates on raw documents and passes them through as raw documents.

removeBOM.help:
Controls the direction of BOM conversion:

- **Enabled**: Removes the BOM from input files if one is detected. By default only UTF-8 BOMs are removed.
- **Disabled**: Adds a BOM to input files if one is not already present. The input files must already be in UTF-8 or UTF-16, and you must specify the encoding of each file so the correct type of BOM is added.
notes: When adding BOMs (option disabled), you **must** specify the encoding of each file so the utility can add the proper type of BOM.

alsoNonUTF8.help: Extends BOM removal to also cover UTF-16 files. Without this, only UTF-8 BOMs are removed.
notes: **Warning:** Removing the BOM from UTF-16 files is **not recommended** — the UTF-16 specification requires a BOM to indicate byte order.

dependsOn.condition: must be true — only meaningful when removing BOMs

limitations: Only UTF-8 and UTF-16 files are supported — UTF-32 is **not** supported.
When adding BOMs, input files must already be encoded in UTF-8 or UTF-16.
processingNotes: Takes and sends **raw documents** — this step does not parse or segment content.
For background on the BOM, see the [Unicode BOM FAQ](http://www.unicode.org/faq/utf_bom.html).

examples.title: Remove UTF-8 BOM
examples.description: Remove the BOM from UTF-8 files only (the most common use case, e.g. fixing files that cause issues with tools that don't expect a UTF-8 BOM).

examples.title: Add BOM to files missing one
examples.description: Add a BOM to UTF-8 or UTF-16 files that don't already have one. Ensure each file's encoding is specified so the correct BOM type is inserted.
